commands.review_summary:
The PR starts instant typing for queued follow-up runs, allows explicit session/default typing configuration to override Telegram room-event suppression, exports `TypingMode`, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +75. Total +98 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source with high confidence: fresh runs signal instant typing while queued runs currently do not, and Telegram room events are suppressed before configured mode resolution. The contributor also gives a concrete live setup, although the after-fix artifact is not inspectable.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No code repair is currently indicated; merge should wait for contributor-supplied inspectable real Telegram proof and normal check completion.

target.title: fix: start typing for queued followup turns and honor configured typingMode for Telegram room events
target.body:
Fixes #92267

## Problem

`agents.defaults.typingMode: "instant"` is silently ineffective for message-tool delivery setups — the configuration the ambient-room-events docs recommend (`unmentionedInbound: "room_event"` + `visibleReplies: "message_tool"`). Two independent gaps:

**Gap 1 — queued/steered turns never start typing.** `createFollowupRunner` builds the typing signaler but never calls `signalRunStart()`; its only trigger is streamed renderable text, which `message_tool` turns never produce (their streamed final is the silent token). With `messages.queue.mode: "steer"` and long-running sessions, most real inbound messages take this path — observed in production as a full day of Telegram traffic with 42 `sendMessage` operations and **zero** `sendChatAction` calls, in DMs and groups alike. `runReplyAgent` already signals run start for fresh turns; the docs define `instant` as "start typing as soon as the model loop begins, even if the run later returns only the silent reply token", with no queued-turn exception.

**Gap 2 — Telegram hardcodes `suppressTyping` for room events.** `bot-message-dispatch.ts` sets `suppressTyping: isRoomEvent` unconditionally while simultaneously setting `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` — the exact mode `resolveTypingMode()` deliberately maps to `"instant"`. The suppression check runs first, so that mapping and any explicit operator-configured `typingMode` are unreachable for unmentioned group turns.

## Fix

- `followup-runner.ts`: call `typingSignals.signalRunStart()` at follow-up run start, mirroring `runReplyAgent`. The signaler gates on mode internally, so `message`/`thinking`/`never` semantics are unchanged; existing `markRunComplete`/`markDispatchIdle` teardown already handles cleanup (silent turns drop typing promptly).
- `bot-message-dispatch.ts`: suppress room-event typing only when the operator never configured a typing mode — `isRoomEvent && resolveConfiguredTypingMode(cfg) === undefined`. The new resolver (session override, then `agents.defaults`) mirrors how `get-reply` resolves the effective mode. Default installs keep today's quiet ambient-room behavior.
- `plugin-sdk/config-contracts.ts`: re-export `TypingMode` for the resolver's signature.

## Tests

- `followup-runner.test.ts`: queued turns start run-start typing under `instant`, and do not under `message` (regression for gap 1).
- `agent-config.test.ts` (new): resolver precedence (unset → undefined, defaults, session override) plus a guard on the existing reasoning-default fallback.

`vitest run` on both touched suites: 79 passed. `tsgo:core` and `tsgo:extensions` clean; oxlint/oxfmt applied.

## Verification beyond tests

Both changes were first applied to a live 2026.6.5 deployment (as dist patches with identical semantics) and verified by the affected user: typing restored in DMs (gap 1 path) and in unmentioned group-topic messages (gap 2 path), with message-tool final delivery unchanged.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Real behavior proof

**behavior:** Telegram typing indicator (`sendChatAction: typing`) now appears during agent runs for (1) queued/steered turns and (2) unmentioned group `room_event` turns, with `typingMode: "instant"` configured. Previously neither path ever issued a `sendChatAction`.

**environment:** Production OpenClaw 2026.6.5, macOS gateway (LaunchAgent), Telegram channel (polling), Codex app-server runtime, `agents.defaults.typingMode: "instant"`, `messages.queue.mode: "steer"`, `messages.groupChat: { unmentionedInbound: "room_event", visibleReplies: "message_tool" }`. Both fixes applied to the live deployment with semantics identical to this PR, gateway restarted.

**steps:**
1. Before fix: operate normally for a day; audit gateway logs for Telegram API operations.
2. Before fix: call the Bot API `sendChatAction` directly with the same token/chat to isolate the layer.
3. Apply both fixes, restart gateway.
4. Send a DM to an agent whose session is mid-run (exercises the follow-up/steered path).
5. Send an unmentioned message in an always-on group topic (exercises the room_event path).

**evidence:** Redacted runtime log audit (before): one day of traffic = 42 `sendMessage` operations, 22 inbound DM/group source messages, **0 `sendChatAction` entries, 0 typing-loop entries**; example DM turn: inbound 17:11:24 → visible reply 17:11:41 with no typing action between. Direct Bot API check (before): `{"ok":true}` from `sendChatAction`, indicator rendered in the client — token/API/UI healthy, isolating the gap to the run pipeline. After fix: typing indicator visibly rendered in the Telegram client within ~1–2s of the inbound message on both paths and persisted through each run until the message-tool reply landed. Screen recording available on request — the deployment is in daily use.

**observedResult:** Both previously-dead paths show live typing through the full run; silent (`NO_REPLY`) turns tear typing down promptly; message-tool final delivery, mentioned-group, and fresh-DM behavior unchanged.

**notTested:** Other channels that share the room-event pattern (e.g. Discord's handler has a similar unconditional flag — out of scope here); `typingMode: "thinking"` under this setup (requires streamed reasoning); heartbeat turns (excluded by design in the signaler).
target.author: binhdoan123
